skill.name: systematic-debugging
skill.description: Use when debugging bugs, test failures, build failures, or unexpected behavior before proposing fixes.
skill.summary: Guide debugging work through root cause investigation, pattern analysis, hypothesis testing, and verified fixes.
skill.author: Cycleaddict

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown examples, TypeScript template strings, and debugging exercises. The confirmed concerns are legitimate diagnostic examples that may expose environment, keychain, filesystem, or local test execution details if used carelessly. No evidence found of malicious intent, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, persistence, or unauthorized network transfer.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Diagnostic Inspection Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes examples that inspect workflow variables, environment state, and macOS keychain signing identities. These examples support debugging, but copied output could expose sensitive operational metadata in logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and environment reads are clearly instructional, not hidden or exfiltrating. Risk remains because users may paste diagnostic output into shared logs or chat.

medium_findings.title: Local Test Execution Helper Script
medium_findings.description: The included Bash helper discovers test files and runs local tests to find state pollution. It does not use shell eval, but it executes repository test code and should only run in trusted worktrees.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly invokes local test execution with quoted arguments and no network transfer. The risk is bounded but real because tests can run arbitrary project code.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Hits Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many Markdown code fences, graph examples, and TypeScript template strings as shell backtick execution. Reviewed context shows these are examples or error strings, not hidden command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain fenced diagrams, polling examples, or string interpolation for timeout messages. No Ruby backtick execution or shell substitution exists at those locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but reviewed lines contain prose, parameter descriptions, or debugging instructions. No MD5, SHA-1, or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing code at the flagged locations. The detector appears to have matched unrelated text rather than cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Static Hits Are Benign Debugging Context
low_findings.description: System and network reconnaissance alerts point to debugging methodology text and pressure-test scenarios. They do not instruct unauthorized scanning or network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss tracing bug causes or choosing a debugging process under pressure. No evidence found of scanning hosts, ports, or systems.

dangerous_patterns.title: Diagnostic Shell Commands in Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation shows shell commands for debugging CI, signing, and test pollution. Users should review commands before running them in sensitive environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible and contextually related to debugging. They still deserve warning because they can reveal local state or run project code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment and Keychain State Inspection
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples reference environment variables and macOS keychain identity inspection. This is useful for debugging signing failures but may disclose sensitive metadata.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is direct, but the intent is diagnostic rather than malicious. The risk depends on where users send the resulting output.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executable Bash Debugging Utility
dangerous_patterns.description: The repository includes a Bash script that runs local tests while checking for filesystem pollution. Treat it as local code execution, not passive documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file is an executable script with direct local command execution. It is simple and quoted, but should be reviewed before use.

content.value_statement: Random fixes waste time and can create new bugs. This skill guides root cause investigation before any fix is proposed.
actual_capabilities: Guides a four-phase process for root cause investigation, pattern analysis, hypothesis testing, and implementation.
Requires consistent reproduction, error review, recent change review, and evidence gathering before fixes.
Provides backward tracing guidance for bugs that appear deep in a call stack.
Shows defense-in-depth validation after the root cause is found.
Replaces arbitrary async waits with condition-based polling patterns.
Includes a local helper script for finding tests that create unwanted state.
limitations: It does not run debuggers, profilers, or tests by itself.
It requires users to provide logs, errors, and reproduction steps.
Its examples are mostly TypeScript, Bash, and CI focused.
Some diagnostic commands can expose sensitive local or CI state.

use_cases.title: Investigate Production Bugs
use_cases.description: Use during incidents to find the failing component before deploying a fix.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Flaky Tests
use_cases.description: Replace sleep-based guesses with condition checks and trace which test creates shared state.

use_cases.title: Review AI Fix Proposals
use_cases.description: Force an assistant to explain evidence, hypotheses, and verification before changing code.

prompt_templates.title: Start Bug Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use systematic debugging for this issue. First read the error, reproduce it, check recent changes, and identify missing evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Root Cause
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace this bug backward through the call chain. Find where the bad value originated before suggesting any fix.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Flaky Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply condition-based waiting to this flaky test. Identify the real condition the test should wait for.

prompt_templates.title: Challenge Repeated Fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Three fixes have failed. Use systematic debugging to decide whether the architecture or pattern is wrong.

output_examples.input: A payment test expects completed status but receives pending status.
output: Phase 1 result: The failure is a state transition mismatch, not a formatting issue.
Evidence needed: Reproduce the timing, inspect recent payment changes, and trace status updates.
Likely investigation path: Compare with working tests that wait for completion events.
Next action: Form one hypothesis after the status update path is understood.

output_examples.input: A test run creates repository metadata in a source folder.
output: Symptom: Unwanted filesystem state appears during tests.
Trace target: Find which test passes an invalid working directory.
Root cause check: Confirm whether setup data is read before test initialization.
Fix direction: Validate the directory at entry, business logic, and environment guard layers.

best_practices: Complete root cause investigation before suggesting a fix.
Test one hypothesis at a time with the smallest useful change.
Add validation at every layer the invalid data crosses.
anti_patterns: Applying a quick fix before reading the full error and stack trace.
Changing multiple variables at once during hypothesis testing.
Using arbitrary sleep delays when a real condition can be observed.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps investigate bugs, test failures, build failures, and unexpected behavior before proposing fixes.

faq.question: Does it execute code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is mostly guidance, but the repository includes a local Bash helper script.

faq.question: Can it help with flaky tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains condition-based waiting and how to find tests that create shared state.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive systems?
faq.answer: Use care. Some examples inspect environment variables, filesystem state, and signing identities.

faq.question: How is it different from test-driven development?
faq.answer: It investigates existing failures first. Test creation appears later to verify the root cause and fix.

faq.question: When should I stop trying fixes?
faq.answer: After repeated failed fixes, stop and question the architecture or pattern before changing more code.
